title: HMW DNA Extraction from Frozen Nematodes
author: Manuela Kieninger

steps.title: Use a worm pellet of about...
steps.instruction: Use a worm pellet of about 40 to 80 mg which was previously flash frozen in liquid nitrogen. More than 100 mg is not recommended as DNA purity gets lower.

steps.title: Per sample
steps.instruction: Per sample: take 600 µL of HMW gDNA Tissue Lysis Buffer and add 20 µL Proteinase K to make the LysisMix buffer. You can prepare a master mix when processing multiple samples. The mix can be prepared at room temperature and then put on ice.

steps.title: Mix well with pipette or vortex
steps.instruction: Mix well with pipette or vortex.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of cold LysisMix buffer to the 1
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of cold LysisMix buffer to the 1.5 mL tube with the frozen nematode pellet. Keep the sample on ice especially during the pestle step.

steps.title: If necessary
steps.instruction: If necessary: transfer the frozen nematode pellet to a Monarch pestle tube; The pellet must be in a 1.5 ml microtube in which the pestle fits.

steps.title: Perform pestle step (at least 30...
steps.instruction: Perform pestle step (at least 30 times up-down and turn the pestle in one direction; not both) It can be necessary to let the pellet thaw a bit in the lysis mix on ice before disruption with the pestle is possible.

steps.title: Add 550 µL LysisMix buffer
steps.instruction: Add 550 µL LysisMix buffer.

steps.title: Transfer all of (~630 µL) the...
steps.instruction: Transfer all of (~630 µL) the sample-LysisMix into a 2 mL LoBind tube and digest at 56°C in the ThermoMixer which is set to 750 rpm for the first 15 min. After 15 min, reduce the rpm to 550 rpm for the rest of the lysis time.

steps.title: Pipette mix well with 1000 µL wide-bore tip
steps.instruction: Pipette mix well with 1000 µL wide-bore tip.

steps.title: Lysis time
steps.instruction: Lysis time: 2 h Can be increased to 4 h if needed. Time for digestion is sample-dependent but 2 h is fine for most nematode species I have processed.

steps.title: Check if worms have fully digested...
steps.instruction: Check if worms have fully digested (longer lysis and more proteinase K can be added if worms are still visible).

steps.title: Possible stopping point
steps.instruction: Possible stopping point: Lysate can be kept in the fridge overnight after this step.

steps.title: Add to ThermoMixer for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Add to ThermoMixer for 10 min at 56°C and 550 rpm.

steps.title: Spin the sample for 5 min...
steps.instruction: Spin the sample for 5 min at 2500 rcf, take the supernatant leaving 30 µL behind. Add the supernatant to a new 1.5 mL LoBind tube. The old tube can be discarded.

steps.title: Add 30 µL of HMW gDNA...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL of HMW gDNA Tissue Lysis Buffer before next step, so the total volume is the same as before the centrifugation.

steps.title: Put sample on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Put sample on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: Add 300 µL cold Monarch Protein...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL cold Monarch Protein Separation Solution and invert right away.

steps.title: Invert at 20 rpm for 1...
steps.instruction: Invert at 20 rpm for 1 to 3 min (wheel or rocker).

steps.title: Leave the sample on ice for 10 min
steps.instruction: Leave the sample on ice for 10 min.

steps.title: Spin at room temperature 16
steps.instruction: Spin at room temperature 16.000 rcf for 25 min or longer if necessary.

steps.title: Check if the sample has two...
steps.instruction: Check if the sample has two stable phases (lower phase can be very small). If the phases are not stable enough for pipetting, you have to centrifuge longer.

steps.title: Take all the upper phase to...
steps.instruction: Take all the upper phase to a new 2 mL LoBind tube. Very slow pipetting is necessary!

steps.title: Add 3 Monarch DNA Capture Beads to the DNA solution
steps.instruction: Add 3 Monarch DNA Capture Beads to the DNA solution. Make sure to add the beads now! Before you add Isopropanol!

steps.title: Add 550 µL Isopropanol and invert...
steps.instruction: Add 550 µL Isopropanol and invert right away after adding several times.

steps.title: Invert on wheel or rocker at...
steps.instruction: Invert on wheel or rocker at 7 rpm for 5 to 7 min.

steps.title: Remove liquid without disturbing the beads...
steps.instruction: Remove liquid without disturbing the beads (HMW DNA should have attached to the glass beads).

steps.title: Keep the supernatant and add it...
steps.instruction: Keep the supernatant and add it to a new microtube in case the DNA did not bind to beads!

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: If the DNA extraction should fail; use the kept supernatant and perform an Isopropanol precipitationto get the DNA out. The supernatant can be saved, and an Isopropanol Precipitation can be done if the DNA extraction fails. I usually add 10 µL of 3 M NaAc pH 5.2 and 200 µL additional Isopropanol and mix well (40x invert). Use a 4 degrees centrifuge here.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Monarch gDNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Monarch gDNA Wash Buffer right away to the tube with the beads.

steps.title: Carefully invert 2 to 3 times
steps.instruction: Carefully invert 2 to 3 times.

steps.title: Remove wash buffer with a tip without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove wash buffer with a tip without disturbing the beads.Add again 500 µL Monarch gDNA Wash Buffer and invert.

steps.title: Remove the wash buffer with a tip
steps.instruction: Remove the wash buffer with a tip.

steps.title: Pour the glass beads to 2...
steps.instruction: Pour the glass beads to 2 mL tube and add 200 µL Monarch gDNA Elution Buffer II. Do not let the glass beads dry for too long.

steps.title: Short spin the tube for 1...
steps.instruction: Short spin the tube for 1 s in a small centrifuge.

steps.title: Check if there is any wool-like...
steps.instruction: Check if there is any wool-like structure in the wash buffer that has been taken away; that happens if the DNA did not stay attached to the beads; you can save this structure (spin and remove liquid) and elute in a separate tube! However, the DNA eluted this way will not be as pure as if it was bound to the beads.

steps.title: Put tube in ThermoMixer at 56°C...
steps.instruction: Put tube in ThermoMixer at 56°C and 300 rpm for 5 min.

steps.title: Put bead retainer in new 1
steps.instruction: Put bead retainer in new 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Pour liquid with beads in retainer...
steps.instruction: Pour liquid with beads in retainer and add 50 µL Monarch gDNA Elution Buffer II on top to wash the beads.

steps.title: Spin in centrifuge for 30 sec at 12
steps.instruction: Spin in centrifuge for 30 sec at 12.000 rcf.

steps.title: Pipette mix 20 times up and down with bore tip
steps.instruction: Pipette mix 20 times up and down with bore tip.

steps.title: Let the DNA elute further in...
steps.instruction: Let the DNA elute further in ThermoMixer for 30 min at 37°C and 300 rpm.

steps.title: Pipette mix well with bore tip...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix well with bore tip and leave the DNA at room temperature overnight for elution.

steps.title: The next day
steps.instruction: The next day: Pipette mix with bore tip at least 20 times. Then do QC: NanoDrop measurement, Qubit measurement, and FemtoPulse run.

steps.title: Add 10 µL RNase A and...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL RNase A and mix well (invert/spin or pipette mix with bore tip).
